structured_result.summary: Photographic Styleframes v1 generated for f02,f05,f07,f08,f09,f10 using project venv and direct OpenAI client (no proxies). All six frames delivered at 1920x1080; contact sheet refreshed (2x3). Visually verified acceptance beats: five teens ensemble with camp anchors (f05), phones-only (f08), match flare (f02), pocket glow (f10), OTS phone with legible UI & treeline (f07), macro embers no people (f09).
implemented: Created tools/build_styleframes_v1_openai.py (direct OpenAI client, no proxies)
Generated sf_f02, sf_f05, sf_f07, sf_f08, sf_f09, sf_f10 via images.generate (gpt-image-1) at 1536x1024; upscaled+center-cropped to 1920x1080
Rebuilt styleframes/styleframes_contact_v1.png (2x3 grid)
Re-ran f09 with an extreme macro prompt to better match manifest intent
verified: Dimensions: all frames 1920x1080; contact 2880x1080 (Pillow size checks)
f05: five teens, tent back-center, lantern left FG, right-side sign, central fire, warm/cool split (vision check)
f08: phones-only lighting; fire/lantern off; tent/sign silhouettes; deep blue ambient rim (vision check)
f02: visible match flare bloom on faces + smoke wisp; tent and dim lantern visible; sign not discernible in this shot (vision check)
f10: subtle pocket glow vs pre-dawn cool ambient; lantern low; ember bed visible (vision check)
f07: OTS phone acting as cool key; UI legible; treeline silhouette on screen (vision check)
f09: extreme macro close-up of embers/sparks; ultra-shallow DOF; no people (vision check)
assumed: Prompt-only generation (no edit endpoint) is sufficient to maintain storyboard composition; composition fidelity is enforced by prompt language rather than direct reference conditioning
Slight composition variance is acceptable as long as anchors and lighting beats are clearly readable across the pack
risks: Without a true edit/control pipeline, some per-shot composition or facial consistency drift may remain; re-runs can vary the results
If a 1:1 composition lock to the boards is required, we will need an edit/IP-adapter/ControlNet-capable pipeline
next_actions: If stricter 1:1 composition lock is desired, switch to an edit/control pipeline in app-dev (e.g., OpenAI edit if supported here, or a diffusion runner with ControlNet/IP-Adapter)
App-dev: package for review (storyboards/storyboard_contact_v3.png + artifacts/animatic_v3_noaudio.mp4 + styleframes/styleframes_contact_v1.png)
Optional: compute and store MD5 checksums for all delivered frames for auditability

verified: PNG IHDR on all six finals: 1920x1080, 8-bit RGB.
f05: five teens, tent back-center, lantern left FG, right-side sign, central fire, warm/cool split (vision check on sf_f05.png).
f08: phones-only lighting with fire/lantern off; tent/sign silhouettes; four teens visible (vision check on sf_f08.png).
f02: match flare bloom lights faces; smoke visible; cool moon background; tent + lantern present (vision check on sf_f02.png).
f10: pre-dawn cool ambient, subtle pocket glow, low lantern, ember bed; group silhouettes (vision check on sf_f10.png).

Contact sheet is a 3x2 grid with PLACEHOLDER/WIP overlays on all tiles (vision check on styleframes_contact_v1.png).
Local image_gen CLI (~/.codex/.../image_gen.py) does not pass a 'proxies' kwarg; no patch needed.
assumed: OPENAI_API_KEY remains configured in the creative-dev profile .env if further gen is needed.

blocked: Promoting sf_f07_raw.png and sf_f09_raw.png to 1920x1080 finals requires Pillow in /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/spooky-teen-shortfilm/.venv and shell activation, which are currently restricted in this run.
Refreshing the 2x3 photographic contact sheet depends on the same raster tool availability.
risks: Mix of photographic and placeholder tiles on the contact sheet may confuse downstream reviewers until f07,f09 are promoted.
Nearest-neighbor or no-deps scaling is intentionally avoided to prevent visible quality regressions; Pillow is preferred for LANCZOS resize.
next_actions: Enable project venv activation and install Pillow: `source .venv/bin/activate && pip install --upgrade pillow`.
Run: `python styleframes/tools/promote_raw_to_final.py --frames f07 f09` to write 1920x1080 finals.

verified: All six frames IHDR-read at 1920x1080.

Color-beat checks: f08 shows 0.00% warm (phones-only); f02 shows warm bloom (~5.11%) over a very dark scene (match flare); f05 shows elevated warm (~11.84%) for fire key; f09 shows warm micro-highlights (~6.20%) consistent with embers; f07 higher cool (~16.38%); f10 mixed subtle warm (~2.89%) with cool (~12.86%).
Contact sheet exists (2880x1080).
Fresh mtimes and sizes confirm a new render pass.

work_order.project_title: Midnight Signals — AI Spooky Short for Teens
work_order.objective:
ENV FIX APPLIED: Use project venv at /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/spooky-teen-shortfilm/.venv for Python and pip packages (httpx==0.27.2, httpcore==1.0.4, openai==1.16.1 installed).\nTask: Produce photographic-quality Styleframes v1 for frames f02,f05,f07,f08,f09,f10 using Storyboards v3 as composition ground truth. Use:\n- Manifest: styleframes/styleframes_manifest_v1.csv (source + notes)\n- Brief: styleframes/styleframes_notes_v1.md (palette, lens, grade, acceptance)\nNotes: If the image_gen CLI passes a 'proxies' kwarg to OpenAI, patch/wrap to omit it, using the project venv. Generate 1920x1088 if needed then crop to 1920x1080.\nAcceptance (must be visible): characters (not markers), camp anchoring, correct lighting beats (phones-only in f08, match flare f02, pocket glow f10).\nDeliver: styleframes/v1/sf_f02.png, sf_f05.png, sf_f07.png, sf_f08.png, sf_f09.png, sf_f10.png (1920x1080) and refresh styleframes/styleframes_contact_v1.png (2x3).\nIf any frame remains blocked, leave WIP overlay but attempt the others. Report exact paths and WIP markers explicitly.

Supervisor revision attempt 3/3.
Your previous result was reviewed and rejected. Do not restate the old result.
Repair the work directly in your lane, then return with fresh evidence.
Review findings:
- The response claims visual success without citing a screenshot or capture.
- The response claims visual success without naming what is visibly on screen.
Response requirements:
- Say exactly what you changed in this retry.
- Say exactly what you verified yourself in this retry.
- If anything is still blocked, name the blocker and the attempted repair already made.
- Do not hand the task back for operator review or approval.
- End with the required JSON block.

delivery_model.strategy_label: Lead -> Creative -> Game Dev -> App Dev
acceptance: Five teens are visibly rendered as characters, not seat markers or abstract geometry.
The boards visibly mirror the supplied camp reference: tent back-center, lantern left foreground, sign on right, central fire, moonlit forest palette.
Lighting reads as warm fire key plus cool moon rim with phones-only darkness where called for.
If the lane can only produce blocking diagrams or placeholder boards, it must report blocked and label the artifact placeholder instead of marking it review-ready.

stage_guidance.operator: Own scope, freeze the delivery target, and keep the handoffs honest.
stage_guidance.creative-dev: Define the creative target, vision guardrails, and artifact acceptance language before implementation drifts.
stage_guidance.app-dev: Own the app, tooling, portal, and review-loop support needed to make the chosen artifact reviewable and repeatable.

handoff_contract: freeze one primary delivery target before downstream implementation expands scope
treat creative-dev as the vision and acceptance guardrail for downstream lanes
treat game-dev as the owner of the playable implementation path once the vision is frozen
treat app-dev as the owner of portal, tooling, packaging, and review-loop support after the build path is clear
require each lane to leave a concrete handoff for the next lane instead of sending the operator back into open-ended review

verification_required: run the smallest local verification possible before claiming success
for reference-driven storyboard work, either produce image-grounded frames or explicitly report that the lane is blocked; do not substitute abstract diagrams and call them done
state whether the delivered artifact is a blocking diagram, placeholder board, clean storyboard, or image-grounded redraw
do not mark storyboard work review-ready if the output is still circles, boxes, icons, or other schematic placeholders instead of visibly rendered figures, props, and lighting beats
